medical_history.chief_complaint: progressive bilateral lower extremity weakness and paresthesias
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The patient reports progressive bilateral lower extremity weakness and paresthesias that started about 1 month ago. She was admitted on ___ for a fall sustained after her legs gave out; at that time, imaging of her spine did not reveal an acute fracture, and etiology of these falls were believed multifactorial, likely from her ___ and complicated by polypharmacy, as well as potentially with some contribution from her diabetes.
medical_history.past_medical_history: T2DM, HTN, HLD, NASH, bipolar disorder, OSA, colon adenoma, compression fractures, depression, diverticulitis, heart murmur, hypertriglyceridemia, hypothyroidism, lupus, obesity, cirrhosis, back pain, carcinoid tumor s/p right lobectomy, arthritis, shoulder dislocation
medical_history.family_history: Daughter and father with depression; father had two courses of ECT with significant improvement of symptoms despite cognitive impairment

physical_examination.general_examination: Gen: NAD, AOx3. Sensation intact in all distributions of the bilateral lower extremities. Perianal sensation intact. Rectal tone intact.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Motor testing results not specified for specific muscle groups.
